title: Inflation and the 2% target
summary: The Bank of England maintains a 2% CPI inflation target set by the Government. Current inflation stands at 3%, above the target, while the Bank Rate is 3.75%. The Bank explains what inflation is, how it is measured, and the consequences of missing the target by more than 1 percentage point either side.
key_points: Current Bank Rate is 3.75% with the next MPC meeting scheduled for 30 April 2026
Current CPI inflation rate is 3%, which is 1 percentage point above the 2% target
The Bank must write an explanatory letter to the Chancellor if inflation deviates more than ±1 percentage point from the 2% target (i.e., above 3% or below 1%)
The ONS measures inflation by collecting around 180,000 prices for approximately 700 items to calculate the CPI
The 2% target helps businesses set prices and households plan spending by keeping inflation low and stable
Governor letters to the Chancellor were sent in December 2025, September 2025, and June 2025 regarding inflation deviations
why_it_matters: The why_it_matters reasonably interprets the Governor letter requirement but overstates the page's significance - this explainer page references those letters rather than containing them.
macro_context: The BoE operates under a symmetric 2% CPI inflation target. With inflation at 3%, the policy framework requires explanation and action. The Bank Rate at 3.75% reflects prior tightening to combat elevated inflation. The April 2026 MPC meeting will be a key date for assessing whether further policy action is needed to return inflation to target.

issues_found: event_type 'monetary_policy_report' is questionable - source is an educational/explainer page describing the inflation target framework, not a substantive policy report
Source is thin on archival value - it explains the existing framework rather than providing new analysis, decisions, or research
Governor letters to the Chancellor (Dec 2025, Sep 2025, Jun 2025) are referenced but not included in this page - the actual substantive policy communications are absent
Published_at derived from 'last updated' (18 Feb 2026) rather than an original publication date; this page has existed much longer
Record describes this page as establishing 'foundational monetary policy framework' which overstates its macro catalyst relevance

human_review.notes: This record is borderline. The factual content is accurate (Bank Rate 3.75%, inflation 3%, 2% target, measurement methodology), but the source is an institutional explainer page rather than substantive macro catalyst content. The event_type classification as 'monetary_policy_report' is questionable - this is more akin to institutional background than a policy report. Given the archive's focus on market structure and macro catalysts, this thin educational content may not warrant archiving when the actual Governor letters are the substantive communications.

notes: Source is a high-trust tier institutional page from the Bank of England explaining their 2% inflation target framework. While the content is factual and sourced from an authoritative central bank, the page is primarily educational rather than providing new macro catalyst information. The most recent actual policy communication appears to be the December 2025 Governor letter to the Chancellor, which is only referenced but not included. The 'published_at' date reflects the page's last update rather than an original publication date. Consider this record as foundational framework context rather than a substantive macro catalyst event.
